select_when: Local optimization is masking the true throughput limiter.
Sequence and capacity matter more than aggregate effort.
Fixing one choke point could unlock disproportionate downstream gain.
A migration, launch, or handoff plan assumes every workstream can move in parallel even though one gating dependency paces the whole system.
avoid_when: The team is labeling the noisiest queue, most visible complaint, or highest-status pain point as the bottleneck without verifying actual throughput.
Constraint analysis is becoming a substitute for execution, or the model is naming the bottleneck without addressing how the constrained step will actually improve.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the curation focused on throughput and gating dependency structure instead of donor breadth across learning and communication uses.
donor_drops: Dropped donor examples that treated bottlenecks as a general complexity lens rather than a throughput-constraint model.
Dropped donor compound-contract hints because higher-order fields remain out for batch 2.
open_questions: The output type merges diagnosis and action pressure, which is operationally useful but still a reviewed normalization.

select_when.note: The raw file gives strong throughput and gating-dependency triggers; donor drafts were trimmed to preserve only the core operational structure.

avoid_when.note: The avoid conditions are synthesized from raw weaknesses about symptom confusion, analysis paralysis, and ignoring implementation.

input_type.note: The raw material clearly concerns constrained flow systems, but the compact input label is a reviewed operational normalization.

output_type.note: The raw model is oriented toward relieving the true constraint; the output type compresses that into an operational intervention label.

reasoning_types.note: The model is clearly systems and causal in use, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
